The first one is to send them not initially to a webinar, but send them to something that they have to opt in to receive and then the webinar comes after that. What that does is that enables you then to be able to market to the people who won't register for the webinar because they've opted in first before they were presented the webinar registration form. And oftentimes, since as we've said before, 70% of people just don't attend webinars. If you don't, if the first place you send them is a webinar signup page, then you're gonna miss that 70% of the audience and you won't be able to come back and market separately outside of the webinar to them. And so that's one reason why I like to have advertising to a webinar. Many cases that advertisement works better, more profitably, if you send them first off to something that they opt in. then I invite them to the webinar. And I haven't found rhyme or reason as to which one works better. is that ads are built on data. And until they get data, they can't make good decision, which means they send you bad traffic. And so it takes a certain amount of spend to get that data.
